title: Sine Wave Simulation
summary: An interactive visualization that demonstrates how sine waves are generated from circular motion 🌀. Originally built with HTML5 Canvas and vanilla JavaScript, later ported to D3.js for richer SVG-based rendering. This was my very first project — the one that sparked my love for coding and creative problem-solving. It holds a special place in my heart as a reminder of where it all began 💛.
contributions: Built the initial prototype using HTML5 Canvas and raw JavaScript to animate circular motion and sine wave generation
Ported the entire visualization to D3.js v7 with SVG rendering for smoother animations and cleaner code architecture
Implemented real-time animated mapping between a rotating point on a circle and the resulting sine wave curve
Visualized core trigonometric concepts — rotating radius vector, swept angle arc, and y-coordinate projection — with color-coded elements
Migrated the codebase to TypeScript and Vite for type safety and a modern dev experience

title: WeldTrace – Smart Welding Manager
summary: A cloud-based fabrication job management platform for weld-mapping, weld tracking, and full lifecycle traceability. Engineers, inspectors, contractors, and welders collaborate in a shared ecosystem to manage WPS/PQR documents, welder qualifications, and project jobs — replacing fragile spreadsheet workflows with a code-compliant, auditable system.
contributions: Built a Weld Annotation Editor enabling engineers to mark up P&ID drawings with precise weld symbols and positional metadata
Designed and implemented the core data model for weld joints, WPS/PQR documents, welder qualifications, and job traceability
Developed a Weld Input Editor — a hybrid data-grid and spreadsheet component for fast, structured entry of weld records at scale
Contributed to multi-role access architecture allowing contractors, project managers, engineers, inspectors, and welders to collaborate within a single project lifecycle

title: WeldQ – Welder Qualification Platform
summary: A full-stack certification platform developed for WTIA (Welding Technology Institute of Australia) to digitise the end-to-end welder qualification lifecycle. WeldQ replaces paper-based processes with a standards-compliant system covering online exam delivery, automated result processing, drag-and-drop certificate design, and long-term credential management — enabling WTIA to certify welders at scale with confidence and compliance.
contributions: Engineered a custom test engine from scratch, supporting timed online exams with question banks, randomisation, and auto-grading for welder qualification assessments
Built an Exam Schedule Editor allowing administrators to plan, configure, and manage certification exam sessions across multiple locations and candidate cohorts
Developed a drag-and-drop Certificate Designer enabling staff to compose branded, standards-compliant qualification certificates populated with live welder data
Implemented a Result Dashboard delivering real-time insights into exam outcomes, pass/fail rates, and per-candidate performance metrics
Designed a document verification and management module to store, validate, and track welder credentials and supporting evidence for regulatory compliance

title: Choice Health Brokers – Internal CRM Platform
summary: A purpose-built internal CRM and communications platform developed from the ground up for a brokerage firm. The platform centralises client and agent management in a single, streamlined workspace — complete with a fully functional in-browser softphone and outbound dialer powered by the Twilio API. Agents can place and receive calls without ever leaving the dashboard, turning a fragmented workflow into a unified, real-time experience built on React and Redux.
contributions: Designed and built a fully interactive editable data grid for managing broker and agent records, supporting inline cell editing, column sorting, and real-time validation without leaving the table view
Integrated the Twilio Programmable Voice API to implement an in-browser dialer, enabling agents to place outbound calls directly from the CRM with call state management driven by Redux

Built a softphone interface for handling inbound call routing in the browser — ring detection, call accept/reject, and live call controls — eliminating the need for external hardware or third-party desk phones
Architected the application from scratch using React and Redux, establishing the component library, state topology, and data flow patterns that the entire platform is built upon
Synchronised real-time call events from the Twilio SDK with UI state to deliver accurate, live status indicators across the dialer, agent list, and activity feed simultaneously
